APPEARANCES - ATTY. P. TERRY ANDERLINI OF ANDERLINI & MCSWEENEY LLP, TEL. NO. 1-650-212-0001 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEPHEN FLEISCHER-IHN OF THE KRONENBERG LAW, TEL. NO. 1-510-254-6763 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER L. CAMPBELL OF THE MARANGA/MORGENSTERN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-248-5315 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CAHILL & KAVANAUGH, INC;
ATTY. LORI R. MAYFIELD, TEL. NO. 1-510-457-9940 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT J.E. BROWN CO. MECHANICAL CONTRACTORS;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER R. BAKER, TEL. NO. 1-310-277-6240 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT LAARS HEATING SYSTEMS CO. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT J.E. BROWN COMPANY MECHANICAL CONTRACTORS'MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF REFILING. The only evidence submitted in support of the motion is a declaration by an attorney, who lacks personal knowledge of the facts of the case and essentially says that she vouches for the good faith of the settlement. A CCP 877.6 motion must be supported by "substantial evidence" showing the nature and extent of the settling defendant's liability as well as a rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery. (Weil and Brown at 12:772 and 12:774-12:774.1). An attorney declaration is usually insufficient, and it is here. (Id.). Ms. Mayfield's declaration provides no useful information on either the nature and extent of J.E. Brown's liability or a rough approximation of plaintiffs' recovery, especially when considered in light of plaintiffs' statement of damages seeking $6,000,000 in non-economic damages. The parties stipulated to continue the trial date from November 7, 2015 to Jan. 30, 2017 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 206. The motion will be heard on December 15, 2016 and the briefing schedule will be in accordance with the code. Parties to meet and confer as to form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROMEL CALIFORNIA, LLC'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF REFILING. The only evidence submitted in support of the motion are: 1) a declaration by an attorney, who provides no facts about the case and essentially says that he vouches for the good faith of the settlement; 2) plaintiffs' interrogatory responses which provide limited information about the extent and nature of plaintiffs' claimed damages; and 3) a declaration submitted by Ben Rubin which provides no information about the extent and nature of plaintiffs' claimed damages. A CCP 877.6 motion must be supported by "substantial evidence" showing the nature and extent of the settling defendant's liability as well as a rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery. (Weil and Brown at 12:772 and 12:774-12:774.1). The evidence submitted by Romel California provides insufficient information on either the nature and extent of J.E. Brown's liability or a rough approximation of plaintiffs' recovery, especially when considered in light of plaintiffs' statement of damages seeking $6,000,000 in non-economic damages. The parties stipulated to continue the trial date from November 7, 2015 to Jan. 30, 2017 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 206. The motion will be heard on December 15, 2016 and the briefing schedule will be in accordance with the code. Parties to meet and confer as to form of order. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHRIS ANDERSEN OF ANDERLINI & MCSWEENEY LLP, TEL. NO. 1-650-202-0001 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PHILLIP T.S. TUKIA OF MARANGA MORGENSTERN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-248-5315 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CAHILL & KAVANAUGH DBA MALCOLM PLUMBING;
ATTY. MELANIE L. O'BRIEN OF KRONENBERG LAW, TEL. NO. 1-510-343-7997 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ROMEL CALIFORNIA, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROMEL CALIFORNIA, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - Defendant Romel California LLC's motion for summary judgment of the claims alleged against it in the complaint and the cross-complaint is denied in its entirety. The existence or nonexistence of a special employment relationship barring an injured employee's lawsuit is generally a question of fact reserved for the trier of fact. (Kowalski v. Shell Oil Co. (1979) 23 Cal.3d 168, 175.) Both plaintiffs and Cahill & Kavanaugh, Inc. presented ample evidence creating a triable issue whether a special employment relationship existed between plaintiffs and Romel. (Plaintiffs' Response to UMF 1, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 16, 18, 20-24; Declarations of Donald Kinn and Dorothy Kinn; Cahill & Kavanaugh, Inc. Response to UMF 1, 8, 9, 10-13, 16, 20-24.) Among other things, plaintiffs and Cahill & Kavanaugh, Inc. submitted evidence disputing whether Romel exercised control over the details of plaintiffs' work, whether plaintiffs' supplied their own tools, whether Romel had the exclusive authority to discharge plaintiffs, and whether plaintiffs consented to an employment relationship with Romel. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
